metadata.title: SincВІ вҶ’ Fermi's Golden Rule
metadata.series: Claude for Educators вҖ” AI1 Simulations

beats.narration_text: Ahoj. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of sincВІ вҶ’ fermi's golden rule. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of sincВІ вҶ’ fermi's golden rule
output: вң“ SincВІ вҶ’ Fermi's Golden Rule
вң“ D3 CDN вҖ” no build step

beats.narration_text: SincВІ вҶ’ Fermi's Golden Rule is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. Transition probability P(Оҙ,T) = (|V_fi|/Д§)ВІ В· sinВІ(ОҙT/2)/(Оҙ/2)ВІ вҖ” as TвҶ’вҲһ, widthвҶ’0, areaвҶ’2ПҖT, densityвҶ’2ПҖВ·Оҙ(Оҙ). This simulation makes that visible вҖ” and every educator can build one.

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: sincВІ вҶ’ fermi's golden rule. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag T slider: from 1 to 100. Watch the physics respond in real time.

beats.narration_text: Transition probability P(Оҙ,T) = (|V_fi|/Д§)ВІ В· sinВІ(ОҙT/2)/(Оҙ/2)ВІ вҖ” as TвҶ’вҲһ, widthвҶ’0, areaвҶ’2ПҖT, densityвҶ’2ПҖВ·Оҙ(Оҙ). Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live demonstration of sincВІ вҶ’ fermi's golden rule. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this SincВІ вҶ’ Fermi's Golden Rule simulation and add a second slider that controls T slider:. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
